content.problemStatement:
Write a C++ program to:


Create a class `Number`
Data member: value
Overload + operator to add two objects
Display result in `main()`
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two space-separated integers a and b.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the sum of a and b on a new line.

solution.approach: The problem requires adding two numbers. We can simply use the '+' operator in our chosen programming language to achieve this. The time complexity is O(1) per test case because we are performing a constant number of operations. The space complexity is also O(1) because we are using a constant amount of space to store our variables.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we are using a constant amount of space.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    a = int(data[idx]); b = int(data[idx+1]); idx += 2
    out.append(str(a+b))
print('\n'.join(out))
code.JavaScript: const lines = require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf-8').trim().split('\n'); let i=0; let T = parseInt(lines[i++]); let out = []; for(let i2=0;i2<T;i2++){ let [a,b] = lines[i++].trim().split(' ').map(Number); out.push((a+b).toString()); } console.log(out.join('\n'));
